title: Asia's refining margins rebound but how sustainable is this emerging recovery?
section_path: Asia's refining margins rebound but how sustainable is this emerging recovery?
section_path_text: Asia's refining margins rebound but how sustainable is this emerging recovery?
summary:
Asia's refining margins are firming somewhat but competition from new Middle East and African refineries could limit any strength. By Serena Huang
Asia's refining margins are showing early signs of recovery in mid-May after a slowdown in the first four months of this year, but any sustained period of strength is far from guaranteed. Robust demand and an expected tightening of clean product inventories in the region amid heavy refinery turnarounds could strengthen margins further in the weeks ahead, but a re-opening of arbitrage options to the West of Suez will be key to lift margins higher.
